summary: Bind the Windows opened executable object to a suspended direct child before resuming it, while keeping the result production-unreachable.
rationale: Issue #369 consumes #368's live local-NTFS opened-object admission. A private owner creates exactly one suspended CreateProcessW child with only three anonymous stdio handles inheritable, derives child-image evidence from the real process handle, compares it to the held executable identity, and resumes only after that comparison. Any failed pre-resume or post-resume promotion path retains kill/reap/handle/lease cleanup privately until it is complete. The public wrapper remains direct-child lifecycle and transport ownership only; no session authority, production caller, browser/provider/CLI path, or handshake is introduced.
deletion_targets: Windows subprocess.Popen fallback or ambient-handle inheritance for the owned sidecar
public pending-process, caller-supplied child-image evidence, or promotion authority
lease release before a failed Windows direct child is proven reaped and native handles are closed
verification: uv run ruff check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev python -m pytest -p no:tach tests/test_owned_sidecar_process.py tests/test_windows_installed_binding.py tests/test_windows_sidecar_process.py -q

uv run --group dev ruff check src/seektalent/owned_sidecar_process.py src/seektalent/windows_sidecar_process.py src/seektalent/windows_installed_binding.py src/seektalent/windows_native_files.py tests/test_owned_sidecar_process.py tests/test_windows_installed_binding.py tests/test_windows_sidecar_process.py
uv run --group dev ty check src/seektalent/owned_sidecar_process.py src/seektalent/windows_sidecar_process.py src/seektalent/windows_installed_binding.py src/seektalent/windows_native_files.py
uv run --group dev python tools/check_privacy_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
risks: Windows Server 2025 CI proves the supported local-NTFS native boundary, not every endpoint filter driver or enterprise policy.
A successful process owner has no authenticated session or workflow authority; the later four-step handshake remains a separate hard cut.
Authenticode, macOS running-image authenticity, complete packaged-sidecar dependency closure, and production composition remain outside this issue.
